archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Top-level orchestration entry point that runs the CI pipeline by spawning subprocesses
Service module that configures MCP servers, permissions, and Claude Code settings during init
Shared utility providing help navigation, typo correction, and keyword search for the n-dx CLI
Adaptive workflow adjustment engine analyzing project metrics and adjusting parameters
Review gate service that presents diffs and prompts for user approval before task completion
Pure functions for detecting agent spin loops — no I/O or side effects
Pure functions for stuck-task detection by counting consecutive failures
Pure data processing functions that aggregate tool call and command records into run summaries
Workflow analysis engine that identifies bottlenecks and optimization opportunities from run history
Backward-compatibility re-export shim for completion validation functions
CLI provider agent loop — orchestrates the full hench run lifecycle for CLI-based LLM providers
Periodic heartbeat writer that keeps lastActivityAt fresh during long-running tool calls
API provider agent loop — orchestrates the full hench run lifecycle for direct API providers
Shared lifecycle helpers (brief assembly, dry run, status transitions) used by both loop variants
Formats and prints task info (title, ID, priority, parent chain) before agent work begins
Pure functions for checking and reporting token budget state
Re-exports token parsing helpers from llm-client with local aggregation utilities
Assembles structured task briefs for the agent by querying the PRD store via rex-gateway
Builds the agent system prompt string from config and project context
Backward-compatibility re-export shim for tool definitions that moved to tools/dispatch
Defines and formats command-specific help content for the hench CLI
Allowlist-based command validation with shell operator and dangerous pattern detection
Type definitions for guard-layer configuration contracts (PolicyLimitsConfig, GuardConfig)
Pure path validation functions for null-byte rejection, escape prevention, and blocked patterns
Stateful session-aware policy engine tracking cumulative resource usage and rate limits
Explicit gateway concentrating all runtime cross-package imports from rex into one auditable module
Time-series metrics collection service for concurrent process counts and resource utilization
Backward-compatibility re-export shim for execShell that moved to tools/exec-shell
Re-exports process execution helpers from llm-client, providing a stable import path for hench consumers
Process lifecycle management service handling orphan detection, cleanup, and resource monitoring
Concurrency limiter controlling process execution rate — shared infrastructure utility
Memory monitoring infrastructure — observes and reports memory state
Memory-based throttling logic — utility wrapping monitor to apply backpressure
Process pool management — shared infrastructure for spawning/reusing child processes
Tracks per-process memory usage — low-level utility supporting the monitor
Queue data structure managing ordered task execution — infrastructure utility
Formatting helpers for queue display/output — pure utility
Priority-based scheduling algorithm — infrastructure utility for queue ordering
Shared glob pattern helpers — cross-cutting file matching utility
Tool interface contracts and type definitions — types companion to tool implementations
Routes tool calls to implementations — dispatcher utility with high fan-in expected
Shell execution tool implementation — utility wrapping child_process for agent use
File system tool operations — utility for agent file read/write/list actions
Git operation tool — utility wrapping git CLI for agent use
Rex integration tool — utility bridging hench agent to rex PRD operations
Shell abstraction layer — utility providing structured shell execution primitives
Test runner tool implementation — utility invoking test suites on behalf of agent
Validates task completion criteria — utility used in agent loop to check done-ness
Implements LLM API provider — service layer handling API calls and response streaming
Authentication helpers (API key resolution, env lookup) — utility supporting providers
Claude CLI-based provider implementation — service layer adapting CLI subprocess as LLM backend
Codex CLI provider implementation — service adapting OpenAI Codex CLI as LLM backend
Factory that selects and instantiates the correct LLM provider — creation utility
Low-level subprocess execution helper — utility shared by CLI providers
Formats help/usage text — pure formatting utility
JSON parsing/streaming helpers — infrastructure utility for LLM response handling
Core LLM client orchestrating provider calls and tool-use loops — primary service module
LLM configuration loading and defaults — config module for vendor/model settings
Shared LLM type definitions and interfaces — types companion to the client
Project-level config reading for LLM settings — config module bridging .n-dx.json to client
Exports a single constant object of directory name strings — pure types/constants, no logic.
Defines the vendor-agnostic LLMProvider interface and related types — pure type definitions.
ProviderRegistry class with dynamic registration and selection logic for LLM providers.
Manages active provider instance lifecycle and vendor switching — session orchestration logic.
Levenshtein edit distance function for CLI typo correction — pure shared utility with no state.
Parses token usage from API responses — stateless transformation utility.
Persists and tracks acknowledged findings with hash computation and store management.
Shared constants (DEFAULT_MODEL, MAX_RETRIES) and ClaudeResult type — companion file to break circular deps.
Post-processing safety net that detects over-granular output and triggers re-consolidation — stateless validation logic.
Recursive decomposition engine that breaks high-LoE items into child proposals.
Merges near-duplicate proposals using similarity scoring — stateless utility with no side effects.
Compares proposals against existing PRD tree to produce diff lines — pure transformation utility.
Parses documents into hierarchical PRD proposals with optional LLM disambiguation — orchestration logic.
Validates file inputs before processing (extension, MIME, size, encoding) — stateless validation helpers.
Interactive Q&A clarification loop with LLM for ambiguous requirements — stateful orchestration.
Holds LLM singleton and spawnClaude bridge — extracted to break circular dependency, but is a service not a re-export gateway.
Takes existing proposals + natural-language modification request and returns revised proposals via LLM.
Defines Proposal, ProposalTask, DuplicateReasonMetadata types and priority ordering — pure data structures.
Main reasoning engine that orchestrates other analyzers; also re-exports for backward compatibility.
Fuzzy-matches proposals against existing PRD items using similarity thresholds — analysis service.
LLM-powered reasoning for PRD reshape proposals (consolidate, move, prune, split).
Scans codebase for requirements in tests, docs, sourcevision output, and package metadata.
Validates natural-language modification requests for specificity before LLM processing — stateless validation.
COMMAND_DEFS record mapping commands to HelpDefinition objects — pure data/constants, no logic.
Implements MCP tool handler functions for all PRD operations — business logic layer between MCP and store.
createRexMcpServer factory and stdio transport setup — CLI entry point for the MCP subcommand.
Centralizes CLI flag parsing and validation functions — shared utility with no state.
Detects existing-item and intra-batch conflicts before PRD creation — analysis service logic.
Validates and atomically creates PRD items from accepted recommendations — orchestration service.
Exports DEFAULT_WORKFLOW string constant containing the default hench agent workflow instructions.
Exports a constant array of archetype definitions with patterns and weights — pure type/constant data, no logic.
Pure heuristic classification functions (major/minor/patch, breaking-change detection) with no side effects.
Orchestrates git diffing and PRD file reading to collect completed branch work items — data fetching/business logic.
Stateless filtering functions for branch-scoped work items based on git lifecycle heuristics.
Persistent JSON read/write store for branch work records with Zod validation — storage layer with high fan-in.
Derives architectural findings (god functions, tight coupling, dead code) from call graph data — analysis business logic.
TypeScript AST-based call graph extraction — stateless analysis service producing structured graph data.
File classification engine scoring archetypes against weighted signals, with optional Claude enrichment — core analysis logic.
Vendor-neutral LLM bridge wrapping @n-dx/llm-client — API client/service abstraction for AI calls.
Reads and aggregates PRD completion status from disk — data access service with graceful degradation.
AST-based React component and route analyzer — stateless analysis service producing structured output.
Generates CONTEXT.md output file — document generation service orchestrating multiple data sources.
Tracks zone metric snapshots across runs and computes convergence reports — analytical service with state comparison logic.
Handles batched AI enrichment with retry logic and prompt construction — orchestration service for LLM calls.
Exports configuration constants (batch sizes, concurrency limits, pass-to-finding-type mappings) for the enrichment pipeline.
Stateless JSON parsing and finding extraction utilities for Claude responses — shared helper functions.
Per-zone concurrent AI enrichment orchestration with incremental re-enrichment support — core pipeline service.
Barrel re-exporter aggregating enrich-config, enrich-batch, claude-client, and enrich-parsing — explicit gateway keeping consumer imports stable.
TSC AST-based import graph extraction with circular detection — stateless analysis service.
Deterministic file discovery and role classification — analysis service producing FileEntry[] output.
Generates structured llms.txt Markdown output — document generation service.
Louvain community detection algorithm implementation for zone grouping — self-contained graph algorithm service.
Manifest read/write with git metadata — data persistence service managing analysis metadata.
Pure analysis logic — detects file misplacement and emits findings via exported functions with no side effects, no IO, no framework coupling. Classic shared utility module.
Converts findings into prioritized agent-ready action steps — business logic orchestration service.
Pure computational module — exports scoring functions, threshold constants, and batch assessment logic for architectural risk. Stateless, framework-agnostic, high fan-in utility.
Detects HTTP routes from JSDoc, Express/Hono/Koa AST patterns — stateless analysis service.
Stateless token accumulation and formatting utilities — shared helper with no side effects.
Aggregates multiple SubAnalysis objects into unified sourcevision output — orchestration service for workspace mode.
Resolves cross-repo import edges into ZoneCrossing entries for workspace aggregation — analytical service.
Analyzer service module orchestrating workspace analysis logic
Utility for computing zone hashes, no direct business logic orchestration
Service that generates zone output files (context.md, summary.json)
Core zone detection/analysis service using Louvain community detection
CLI command handler for the MCP subcommand
CLI command handler for the serve subcommand
Service module that generates PDF report exports
Template/generator utility for PR markdown output
Canonical JSON sorting utility used across output generation
Build configuration/script for the web package
Dev server configuration/script for the web package
In-process cache service for aggregated data
Gateway module concentrating all sourcevision cross-package imports for the web server
Service computing incremental task usage/token analytics
Service providing diagnostics for PR markdown refresh operations
Service handling PRD file I/O operations
Service that tracks process memory usage over time
Service that registers and manages scheduled tasks
Gateway module concentrating all rex cross-package imports for the web server
Express route handler for rex analysis endpoints
Express route handler for rex execution endpoints
Express route handler for health check endpoints
Express route handler for rex item CRUD endpoints
Express route handler for rex prune endpoints
Exports routePrdReads() — Express GET route handlers for /api/rex/prd and /api/rex/stats endpoints.
Exports routeItemRequirements() — Express CRUD route handlers for /api/rex/items/:id/requirements.
Shared helpers (findItemById, loadPRD, savePRD) and constants used across Rex route handler files — infrastructure utilities, no routes of its own.
In-memory search index service — lazy-built inverted index with mtime-based invalidation; business logic layer, not a route handler.
Pure type definitions (TaskUsageAccumulator, CollectAllIdsFn, OrphanedEntry, CleanupResult) with no runtime code.
Server entry point — creates HTTP server, wires all routes, writes port file; top-level bootstrap with many uncalled side-effect exports.
Explicitly described as a public facade — re-exports from internal sub-modules (aggregation, cleanup scheduling, registration) with no added logic.
Periodic background service — schedules orphaned usage record cleanup, cross-references PRD state, writes audit log.
WebSocket server implementation — manages connections, broadcasts, ping/pong keepalive; infrastructure service layer.
Pure constants — DATA_FILES, ALL_DATA_FILES, SUPPLEMENTARY_FILES — no runtime logic.
Off-screen DOM node culling engine using IntersectionObserver — shared infrastructure utility with no framework coupling.
Application bootstrap — module-level side effects for theme, tab-visibility, polling infrastructure; called once at startup.
Explicitly documented as the viewer-layer gateway — re-exports schema types, DATA_FILES constants, and createRequestDedup with no logic.
Pure physics engine (Barnes-Hut quad-tree, force-directed layout) — stateless computational utilities extracted from graph renderer.
GraphRenderer class — imperative SVG rendering orchestration owning DOM manipulation, event handlers, LOD, and physics integration lifecycle.
Data loader service — fetches and validates .sourcevision/ JSON files with lazy parallel loading; API client / data fetching layer.
Rate limiter with queue deduplication — reusable infrastructure utility that wraps any async function with throttling behavior.
Composed fetch pipeline (rate limiter + request dedup) — wraps async functions; pure infrastructure utility, no business logic.
WebSocket message coalescing — batches rapid sequential messages into single flush; reusable messaging infrastructure utility.
Per-type WebSocket message throttle with trailing-edge debounce — reusable messaging infrastructure utility.
In-flight request deduplication — wraps async functions so concurrent callers share one promise; canonical location for this utility.
Composed WebSocket message pipeline (throttle → coalescer) — reusable two-layer messaging infrastructure utility.
Client-side crash detection service — manages sessionStorage heartbeat, crash history tracking, loop detection, and navigation state recovery.
DOM performance monitoring service — tracks active node counts, render timing, and memory usage with before/after comparison.
DOM update gate — wraps UpdateBatcher to intercept schedule() calls on tab visibility; reusable infrastructure utility.
Graceful degradation manager — subscribes to memory monitor and progressively disables features; stateful orchestration service.
Memory monitoring service — polls performance.memory, tracks usage trends, emits warnings; stateful background service.
Memory-aware refresh throttling service — intelligent scheduling with concurrent request management based on memory pressure levels.
Response buffer gate — suspends/resumes message pipeline on tab visibility; composable infrastructure utility complementing polling-manager.
Standalone RAF-based batching module with no framework dependencies; provides scheduling/flush/dispose infrastructure consumed by other modules.
Orchestrates tick subscriptions and batched RAF state updates across multiple consumers; acts as a coordination service between tick-timer and hook instances.
Centralized registry managing polling interval lifecycle with tab-visibility integration; coordinates multiple pollers as a service layer.
Bridges graceful-degradation system to polling-state; coordinates suspend/resume of all polling sources in response to memory pressure events.
Central registry with shared mutable state (sources map, generation counter, listeners) that multiple modules read/write; high fan-in state management module.
Wraps Page Visibility API with subscriber pattern, state tracking, and transition history; provides a shared browser API abstraction service.
Shared setInterval service with auto-lifecycle (start on first subscriber, stop on last); coordinates tick distribution across consumers.
Bridges tab-visibility to tick-timer suspend/resume lifecycle; a coordination service with factory pattern and no framework dependencies.
Applies schema version migrations to data modules; contains transformation logic and a migration registry.
Path display helpers plus backward-compatible re-exports from visualization layer; shared utility module with high fan-in.
Zone color palette and lookup helpers — pure functions with a constant palette; focused visualization utility.
Pure data-transformation functions that build edge/node arrays for flow diagrams from zone/call/import data.
Single pure helper function classifying metric values for meter display; minimal focused utility.
Implements the PR validation pipeline (build + rex validate) as an exported runPRCheck function with a CLI entry point; orchestration service module.
Exported service function that refreshes sourcevision dashboard artifact metadata files; data-layer service helper.
Translates refresh CLI flags into a validated execution plan; pure business logic service with no I/O.
Snapshot, validation, and rollback helpers for refresh operations; stateful service coordinating file I/O for refresh lifecycle management.
Server orchestration module exporting runWeb and PID/port management functions; used by cli.js to start/stop/query the web server.
